channel.title: MCP News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated news about Model Context Protocol, AI agents, tool integrations, MCP security, and developer tooling.

item.title: I Built a Live Registry of 750+ MCP Servers — Here's What I Found
item.description: I Built a Live Registry of 750+ MCP Servers — Here's What I Found [Draft for dev.to / HN — Chris to review before posting. Add author bio at bottom.] Model Context Protocol (MCP) has exploded in the past six months. Every week, dozens of new MCP servers appear on GitHub — tools that let Claude and...
item.dc:creator: Kiprio

item.title: Power Up Your Claude Workflows with the Kiprio MCP Server
item.description: dev.to Article Draft: Using Kiprio MCP Server in Your Claude Workflows Power Up Your Claude Workflows with the Kiprio MCP Server claude, mcp, api, developer, ai If you're using Claude Desktop or Claude Code and want to add real-world data capabilities to your AI workflows, the Kiprio MCP server...
item.dc:creator: Kiprio

item.title: How to build your first MCP server in 10 minutes
item.description: I built my first MCP server last week and it was way simpler than I expected. Here is exactly how, no fluff. Prerequisites Node.js 20+ 10 minutes Step 1: Scaffold npx create-mcp-server my-first-server cd my-first-server npm install This generates a complete TypeScript project with one example tool....
item.dc:creator: GrahamduesCN

item.title: Monitoring MCP in Production: Server and Client Metrics That Matter
item.description: The Model Context Protocol, commonly known as MCP, gives AI applications a standardized way to connect with external tools, APIs, databases, files, and other data sources. A typical MCP interaction looks simple: User → AI application or agent → MCP client → MCP server → Tool or resource → Database,...
item.dc:creator: Anuj Tyagi

item.title: I Self-Hosted SigNoz for a Hackathon — Here's What the Docs Don't Cover
item.description: I sat down on a Saturday to self-host SigNoz for the Agents of SigNoz hackathon. The official Docker guide is clear: install foundryctl, write a casting.yaml, run foundryctl cast. Three commands, containers up, UI on :8080. Done. Except not done. The docs get SigNoz running. They don't get a...
item.dc:creator: Rahul R M

item.title: I built an ad layer that can never break a tool call — monetizing MCP servers
item.description: Disclosure: I'm the founder of Lulu Ads. This is the engineering story — the interesting parts are the constraints, and I'd rather you tear the design apart than clap. The problem nobody is paid to solve There are thousands of MCP servers right now — the tools that AI agents like Claude call to...
item.dc:creator: Tal Mogendorff

item.title: The night four MCP servers started acting like one system
item.description: Last weekend I watched an agent workflow do something I had been waiting to see outside a demo. Hermes needed to hand a detailed review to another coding agent, start a fairly heavy Codex job, avoid overloading the local server, and leave enough evidence for me to check what happened afterwards. It...
item.dc:creator: Stephen Phillips

item.title: Building an AI-Ready SaaS Discovery Engine with Next.js, PostgreSQL, and MCP
item.description: Software discovery used to be a straightforward search problem. A user typed a category, opened a few result pages, compared features, and selected a product. That flow is changing. Today, a software product may be discovered through a search engine, a curated directory, an AI assistant, a...
item.dc:creator: Jack M

item.title: CodexPro: ChatGPT as a Coding Agent Scoped to One Repo
item.description: CodexPro turns ChatGPT's Developer Mode into a coding agent that touches only one local repo, and it draws a hard line at what that agent is allowed to do. The README spends as much space telling you what the tool refuses to be as it does explaining what it does: "not a hosted service, model proxy,...
item.dc:creator: Reno Lu

item.title: Build an MCP Nutrition Server
item.description: Build an MCP server for nutrition data An MCP server gives an AI client a narrow, inspectable nutrition lookup tool instead of asking the model to recall product labels from memory. Wrapping DietlyAPI in the Model Context Protocol lets Claude, Cursor and other MCP hosts fetch real macros on demand,...
item.dc:creator: Dietly

item.title: My Comment-Reply Bot Hit a Wall the Docs Never Mentioned. That Wall Turned Out to Be a Security Feature.
item.description: I run a small MCP server (server.py in this repo) that already talks to the DEV.to API to publish articles — create_article POSTs to /api/articles with my key, works every time. So when I found, almost by accident, that 35 published articles had accumulated 20 unanswered comments (some six weeks...
item.dc:creator: Enjoy Kumawat

item.title: x402 processed 169M payments. On my eight MCP servers: zero. Only the scouts arrived.
item.description: Part of a series on building cz-agents → under the hood. Where we left off In June, I compared the three camps of agentic payments here—x402, card tokens, and banks—and argued that x402 will take machine-to-machine micropayments, while cards and banks split the rest. I won't repeat the basics of...
item.dc:creator: Martin Havel

item.title: Beyond text: Returning images and interactive apps from MCP servers
item.description: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) is becoming a richer foundation for agent experiences. Though most servers return plain text from their tool calls, MCP servers can also return binary results and provide interactive apps in clients that support those features, like VS Code. In this post, I'll use...
item.dc:creator: Pamela_Fox

item.title: Agent Runs now available in the Vercel MCP and CLI
item.description: Your agent can now inspect Agent Runs via the Vercel MCP and CLI for eve, the open-source agent framework.eve traces are automatically ingested when deployed to Vercel and available as Agent Runs. The new Vercel MCP tools and Vercel CLI commands let you find projects with runs, list recent runs,...
item.dc:creator: Brooke Mosby

item.title: Announcing the Monetization Gateway: charge for any resource behind Cloudflare via x402
item.description: We're opening the waitlist for our Monetization Gateway, which will allow you to charge for any web page, dataset, API, or MCP tool behind Cloudflare. The charges will settle in stablecoins over the x402 open protocol, with no payments stack of your own to build.
item.dc:creator: Will Papper

item.title: Beta SDKs for the 2026-07-28 MCP Spec Release Candidate Are Here
item.description: Beta releases of the Python, TypeScript, Go, and C# SDKs are now available with support for the 2026-07-28 MCP specification release candidate. Here is what changes for your server, how to migrate, and how to test before the spec goes final on July 28.
item.dc:creator: Model Context Protocol

item.title: MCP for Beginners: Why Every AI Engineer and Developer Should Learn the Model Context Protocol
item.description: If you have spent any time building with large language models in the last year, you have hit the same wall everyone hits: your model is brilliant at reasoning but blind to the real world. It cannot read your database, call your internal API, search your documents, or trigger a deployment unless...
item.dc:creator: Lee_Stott

item.title: MCP Server Authorization with Azure API Management: From Simple to Advanced
item.description: Why put API Management in front of your MCP servers The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has quickly become the standard way for AI agents, such as GitHub Copilot in VS Code, to reach external tools and data. As soon as an MCP server does anything meaningful, the same questions that govern any API...
item.dc:creator: vzisiadis
